title: Bothriocline monticola
taxonomy: Bothriocline monticola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Bothriocline, which is a group of plants commonly known as blue plums or mountain blue plums. Bothriocline monticola is native to the mountainous regions of China and is closely related to other species in the Bothriocline genus. The plant is characterized by its small, blue-purple flowers and hairy leaves. Through its classification, Bothriocline monticola can be identified as a unique species within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Bothriocline monticola, commonly known as Mountain Plumbago, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Bothriocline monticola include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
